name: Maple Sugar Bread
recipeIngredient: 6 medium tart cooking apples (peeled and cut into 1/2" lengthwise slices)
recipeInstructions:
1. Combine apples and water in a 2 or 3 quart enameled saucepan. Bring to a boil over high heat.
2. Reduce heat to low and simmer, partially covered, for about 20 minutes, or until the apples are soft and can be mashed easily against the side of the pan with the back of a spoon.
3. Puree apples through a food mill or rub them through a fine sieve into a bowl. Measure and reserve 1 1/2 cups of the puree; save any extra puree for another use.
4. Preheat the oven to 350°F.
5. Drop hazelnuts into enough boiling water to immerse them completely. Cook briskly, uncovered, for 2 minutes. Drain nuts in a sieve and peel while still hot using a small, sharp knife.
6. Spread nuts in a baking dish and toast in the middle of the oven for about 10 minutes, stirring occasionally so they color delicately on all sides.
7. Coarsely chop hazelnuts and set aside.
8. Combine flour, baking soda, cinnamon, nutmeg, and salt on a plate or sheet of wax paper.
9. Add raisins to a bowl and mix with about 1/4 cup of the flour mixture.
10. Coat the bottom and sides of a 9x5x3 loaf pan with softened butter.
11. If maple sugar is moist, grate it on the finest side of a stand-up hand grater; if dry, grate using a nut grinder. Grate about 1 1/2 cups of maple sugar.
12. In a large bowl, cream together 1/2 cup softened butter and grated maple sugar with a large spoon until light and fluffy (or use an electric mixer).
13. Add eggs one at a time, beating well after each addition.
14. Beat in about 2/3 cup of the flour mixture, then about half of the apple puree. Repeat two more times until all of the flour mixture and apple puree are added.
15. Stir in hazelnuts and raisins, then pour the batter into the buttered pan, spreading and smoothing the top with a spatula.
16. Bake in the middle of the oven for 1 1/2 hours, or until the top is golden brown and a toothpick inserted in the center comes out clean.
